oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Tack this out! Practical tips for LDA surgery in dairy cows
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Forrestal, Amber
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: left displaced abomasum
dairy cow surgery
right flank pyloropexy
right paramedian abomasopexy
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Left displaced abomasum (LDA) is a common surgical condi­tion encountered in dairy cattle, particularly during early lactation. Although multiple surgical techniques are avail­able for correction, consistent success depends on more than technical execution alone. Surgeon physical size, appropriate technique selection, effective restraint, anatomical knowledge and thorough postoperative management all play critical roles in surgical outcomes. The objective of these proceedings is to present practical, field-applicable tips for successful correc­tion of LDA in dairy cows. Both the right flank pyloropexy and right paramedian abomasopexy techniques are reviewed, with emphasis on the advantages and limitations of each. Detailed guidance is provided for casting and positioning for the right paramedian abomasopexy. Relevant anatomy is discussed to improve surgical accuracy and efficiency. Additional consider­ations addressed include surgeon physical limitations, incision placement and size, abomasal deflation techniques, chemical and physical restraint strategies, and tips for administering lo­cal anesthetic blocks. Postoperative management focusing on metabolic support analgesia is emphasized as an essential com­ponent of success. By incorporating these practical strategies, veterinarians can improve surgical efficiency, reduce complica­tions, enhance cow comfort, and achieve more consistent out­comes when correcting left displaced abomasum under chal­lenging field conditions.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 65-70
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
